summary: Optimize the auto-mount property search order from global scene-wide DFS to a nearest-first two-phase strategy. Phase 1 searches downward from the current node's subtree. Phase 2 walks upward through ancestors, searching each ancestor's full subtree (excluding already-visited branches), stopping at the first ancestor level that yields a match.
approach: Minimal-change approach: (1) Modify scene-accessor.js to pass the current node (already available) instead of scene root to autoMountProperties. (2) Modify property-mounter.js autoMountProperties to implement two-phase search per property using the existing traverseNodeTree with a shared visited Set. (3) Remove visited cleanup in traverseNodeTree so the Set persists across multiple calls within the same property search. No new configuration options needed - direct replacement of search strategy.

design_decisions.decision: Reuse existing traverseNodeTree with shared visited Set for two-phase search
design_decisions.rationale: Minimizes code changes and leverages existing cycle detection and depth guards
design_decisions.tradeoff: Requires removing visited.delete() which changes traverseNodeTree cleanup behavior, but no other callers are affected
alternatives: Add proximity bonus to scoring system

design_decisions.decision: Direct replacement of search strategy with no configuration option
design_decisions.rationale: User explicitly chose simplest approach; nearest-first is universally better UX for auto-mount
design_decisions.tradeoff: Users who relied on global DFS order for distant node matching will get different results
alternatives: Keep global as default, nearest-first as opt-in

design_decisions.decision: Stop search at first ancestor level that finds any match
design_decisions.rationale: Simple, predictable behavior - user explicitly chose this over proximity scoring or threshold-based approaches
design_decisions.tradeoff: May miss a higher-scoring match at a more distant ancestor level
alternatives: Add proximity bonus to scores globally
Stop only if match score >= threshold
